title: Architecture Confronts Climate Crisis
subtitle: 建筑如何应对全球变暖的挑战

paragraphs.en: The construction industry accounts for nearly forty percent of global carbon emissions, making sustainable architecture crucial to climate action. Buildings consume vast energy for heating, cooling, and lighting throughout their lifetimes. Architects are now reimagining design principles to minimize environmental impact from construction through demolition.
paragraphs.zh: 建筑业占全球碳排放量的近40%，使可持续建筑对气候行动至关重要。建筑物在其整个生命周期中消耗大量能源用于供暖、制冷和照明。建筑师们现在正在重新构想设计原则，以最大限度地减少从建造到拆除的环境影响。

paragraphs.en: Passive design strategies reduce energy needs by working with natural forces rather than against them. Building orientation maximizes beneficial solar exposure while minimizing unwanted heat gain. Natural ventilation, thermal mass, and strategic shading can dramatically reduce dependence on mechanical climate control systems.
paragraphs.zh: 被动式设计策略通过顺应而非对抗自然力量来减少能源需求。建筑朝向最大化有益的日照同时最小化不需要的热量增加。自然通风、热质量和战略性遮阳可以显著减少对机械气候控制系统的依赖。

paragraphs.en: Material choices significantly impact a building's carbon footprint. Concrete production alone generates eight percent of global emissions. Architects increasingly specify low-carbon alternatives like mass timber, recycled materials, and innovative composites. Some experimental buildings even sequester carbon through biological materials that continue growing after installation.
paragraphs.zh: 材料选择显著影响建筑的碳足迹。仅混凝土生产就产生了全球8%的排放。建筑师越来越多地指定低碳替代品，如大型木材、回收材料和创新复合材料。一些实验性建筑甚至通过安装后继续生长的生物材料来封存碳。

paragraphs.en: Retrofitting existing buildings presents both challenges and opportunities. Most structures that will exist in 2050 have already been built, making renovation essential to climate goals. Energy-efficient upgrades to insulation, windows, and mechanical systems can transform old buildings into high-performance structures.
paragraphs.zh: 改造现有建筑既带来挑战也带来机遇。到2050年将存在的大多数建筑已经建成，这使得翻新对气候目标至关重要。对隔热、窗户和机械系统进行节能升级可以将老旧建筑转变为高性能建筑。

paragraphs.en: The most forward-thinking architects design buildings that give back to their environments. Net-positive structures generate more energy than they consume through integrated solar panels and efficient systems. Green roofs and facades support biodiversity while managing stormwater and improving air quality. Architecture becomes not merely less harmful but actively regenerative.
paragraphs.zh: 最具前瞻性思维的建筑师设计的建筑能够回馈环境。净正效益建筑通过集成太阳能电池板和高效系统产生的能源超过其消耗。绿色屋顶和立面支持生物多样性，同时管理雨水并改善空气质量。建筑不仅仅变得更少伤害，而是主动具有再生性。
